summary: Implemented the M3 appointment workspace as a React DOM/client read-model feature: client-core now exposes appointment, character, polity/vassal, obligation, succession/vacancy, eligibility, bulk preview, result, and reason-summary slices projected from protocol query DTOs; UI renders appointment and bulk command workflows with per-candidate rejection reasons, policy continuity, administrative impact preview, succession/obligation/result panels, and command DTO submission callbacks without mutating authoritative state.

tests_run.result: Vitest passed 1 file / 8 tests, including M3 appointment read-model projection, rejection reason, and command DTO coverage.
Vitest SSR passed 1 file / 4 tests, including M3 appointment, bulk, rejection reason, succession, obligation, result, and reason summary rendering.
Web shell Vitest passed 1 file / 3 tests, including bootstrapped M3 appointment slice smoke coverage.
Vite production web build passed; emitted existing chunk-size warning for lib-DHa59PjR.js over 500 kB.
Architecture boundaries passed; sim determinism/save-load hashes matched; root Vitest passed 24 files / 161 tests; web Vitest passed 1 file / 3 tests.
Chromium Playwright passed 3 tests, including the M3 appointment workspace command submission flow.
Prettier check, ESLint, recursive typecheck, and architecture boundary check passed.

tests_run.command: node -e JSON parse/shape check for M3-CLIENT-APPOINTMENT-UI-001
tests_run.result: Parsed task JSON and verified id, status, allowed_paths, and required_tests shape.
Showed only task-scoped modified files and the new M3 writer handoff file.

decisions: Used Playwright coverage instead of Storybook because root .storybook is outside this task's allowed_paths and apps/storybook does not exist in this checkout.
Kept M3 UI as React DOM over client read-model slices; no Pixi ownership, Electron, Node, or authoritative WorldState mutation was introduced.
Projected visible eligibility and reason summaries from protocol query/read-model DTO-shaped inputs and command result reason codes; UI does not copy sim-core appointment formulas or hidden weights.
Generated sim.appoint-office and sim.appoint-offices-bulk DTOs through client-core command builders and surfaced them via a Web shell callback/status instead of applying them locally.
risks: Current M3 appointment workspace uses a protocol DTO projection fixture because there is not yet a single live worker query adapter feeding the page-level appointment workspace in apps/web.

Existing protocol query output exposes office/enfeoffment/succession/postwar reason data but not a complete live character catalog or per-office candidate eligibility query; this UI keeps those values as read-model fixture inputs and should be wired to a future systems_architect-approved query rather than duplicating sim-core rules.
Build still reports the pre-existing Vite chunk-size warning for a large generated lib chunk; no new production dependency was added.
requested_action: Review M3 appointment UI/read-model implementation, Playwright coverage, boundary compliance, and the noted live-query integration risk.
